title: Icy Japanese Leek Ochazuke
description: A soothing, cold version of the traditional Japanese comfort dish, featuring leek and deep-fried tofu, perfect for hot summer days in Hokkaido.
instructions: Chill the deep-fried tofu by placing it in the refrigerator overnight or freezing it for at least 2 hours.
Chill the Japanese green tea in the refrigerator before serving.
Slice the leeks into 1-inch pieces, separating the white and green parts.
Deep-fry the tofu according to the package instructions, then let it cool to room temperature.
Chill the tofu in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving.
Just before serving, place the tofu, leeks, and a few ice cubes in a bowl.
Pour the chilled Japanese green tea over the tofu and leeks, and stir in the wasabi, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil.
Sprinkle the toasted sesame seeds and salt over the top of the dish.
Garnish with shredded daikon and shiso if desired.
Serve immediately, while the ice is still in the bowl.
